nation: Madawaska Maliseet First Nation New Brunswick
one_time_description: Other items (note 21) - large non-recurring item reducing surplus by $72,831,511 in 2023 (vs $96,455,793 credit in 2022); also $1,000,000 loss on investment in BeeHigh Vital Elements Inc. written off
economic_notes: Band operates St. John Valley Gas Bar, Smoke Signal (tobacco/merchandise), and Madawaska Maliseet Economic Development Corporation. Investment in BeeHigh Vital Elements Inc. (licensed medical marijuana producer, 15.78% ownership) written off at $1,000,000. Video lottery terminal and gaming agreement revenue of $2,471,020. Provincial tax rebates of $15,433,255.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED for three reasons: (1) budgeted amounts not prepared in accordance with PSAB standards, (2) investment in BeeHigh Vital Elements Inc. (private medical marijuana company) carried at cost with possible impairment not determined - written off $1,000,000 in 2023, (3) leases on leased housing accounted for as operating leases rather than finance leases. Large 'Other items' of -$72,831,511 in 2023 and +$96,455,793 in 2022 (Note 21 not fully visible) caused major swings in accumulated surplus. Treaty credit paid to band members $6,649,000. Three band-owned businesses reported in annexes. Provincial tax rebate agreement ended January 31, 2023. Total government transfers calculated as federal operating + federal capital + provincial operating = $3,705,262 + $357,330 + $259,115 = $4,321,707 (excludes CMHC, Health Canada etc. which are included in expense schedules as subsidized programs).
